# kDGLM: An R package for Bayesian analysis of Generalized Dynamic Linear Models

Welcome to the `kDGLM` GitHub repository! `kDGLM` is an innovative R
package tailored for Bayesian analysis of Dynamic Generalized Linear
Models (DGLM), catering to both uni- and multivariate exponential
families. This package is your go-to tool for sequential inference on
time series data, offering a broad spectrum of functionalities including
fitting, smoothing, monitoring, and feed-forward interventions.

Developed based on the methodology proposed in Alves et
al.([2024](https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2201.05387)), `kDGLM`
integrates seamlessly with established techniques in the literature,
particularly those applied in Gaussian Dynamic Models. It supports
various features such as discount strategies, autoregressive components,
transfer functions, and more, leveraging the Kalman filter and smoothing
properties for exceptional computational efficiency. With kDGLM, you can
expect almost instantaneous fitting times that scale linearly with your
time series length, making it an invaluable tool for analyzing extended
time series datasets.

Currently, `kDGLM` supports a variety of distributions, including:

- Univariate Normal (with unknown mean and observational variance);
- Bivariate Normal (with unknown means, observational variances, and
  correlation);
- Poisson;
- Gamma (with known shape and unknown mean);
- Multinomial (with known number of trials and unknown event
  probabilities).

Furthermore, `kDGLM` allows for the joint modeling of multiple time
series, provided each series adheres to one of the supported
distributions. Our ongoing development efforts are focused on
continuously expanding the range of supported distributions to enhance
the package’s versatility.

Whether you are analyzing time series data for research, business
intelligence, or any other purpose, `kDGLM` offers a powerful,
efficient, and comprehensive solution for your needs. Explore our
documentation to get started and join the community of users benefiting
from the advanced capabilities of `kDGLM`!

# Installation

## Dependencies

Before installing kDGLM, ensure you have the following dependencies
installed in your R environment:

- `extraDistr` \>= 1.9.1
- `cubature` \>= 2.1.0
- `Rfast` \>= 2.0.8
- `generics` \>= 0.1.3

Additionally, for an enhanced plotting experience, we recommend
installing the `ggplot2` and `plotly` packages, which augment the
built-in plot methods provided by `kDGLM`.

## Installing kDGLM

As of now, `kDGLM` is available directly from its GitHub repository.
While we are in the process of submitting `kDGLM` to CRAN, you can
install the latest version of the package by executing the following
command in your R console:

``` r
remotes::install_github('silvaneojunior/kDGLM')
```

This command uses the remotes package to install kDGLM. If you do not
have remotes installed, you can install it first by running
install.packages(“remotes”).

# Getting Started

After installation, load `kDGLM` into your R session:

``` r
library(kDGLM)
```

To test if the package is working properly, execute the following code
exemple:

``` r
 # Poisson case
 data <- c(AirPassengers)

 level <- polynomial_block(rate = 1, order = 2, D = 0.95)
 season <- harmonic_block(rate = 1, order = 2, period = 12, D = 0.975)

 outcome <- Poisson(lambda = "rate", data = data)

 fitted.data <- fit(level, season,
   AirPassengers = outcome
 )
 summary(fitted.data)

plot(fitted.data, plot.pkg = "base")
```

Refer to the [vignettes](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/) for
detailed usage instructions. Also see the documentation of the `fit`
method for basic examples for each type of outcome the `kDGLM` package
offers.

# Contributing

We welcome contributions! If you’re interested in improving kDGLM,
please consider submitting bug reports, feature requests, or pull
requests.

# Acknowledgements

- Raíra Marotta, who gently provided the base code for the initial
  versions.

# Contact

For support or to provide feedback, please contact [Silvaneo dos Santos
Jr.](mailto:silvaneo@dme.ufrj.br) or open an issue on the GitHub
repository.

# Package index

## All functions

- [`block_mult()`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/block_mult.md)
  : Auxiliary function to replicate blocks
- [`block_rename()`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/block_rename.md)
  : block_rename
- [`block_superpos()`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/block_superpos.md)
  : Auxiliary function for block superposition
- [`CAR_prior()`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/CAR_prior.md)
  : CAR prior
- [`chickenPox`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/chickenPox.md)
  : Hospital admissions by chicken pox in Brazil
- [`coef(`*`<fitted_dlm>`*`)`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/coef.fitted_dlm.md)
  : coef.fitted_dlm
- [`cornWheat`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/cornWheat.md)
  : Corn and wheat prices from 1986 to 2014
- [`ffs_block()`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/ffs_block.md)
  [`ffs()`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/ffs_block.md)
  : Structural blocks for free-form seasonal trends and regressions
- [`fit_model()`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/fit_model.md)
  : Fitting kDGLM models
- [`fit_model_single()`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/fit_model_single.md)
  : Fitting one kDGLM models
- [`forecast(`*`<fitted_dlm>`*`)`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/forecast.fitted_dlm.md)
  : Auxiliary function for forecasting
- [`Gamma()`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/Gamma.md)
  : Gamma outcome for kDGLM models
- [`gastroBR`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/gastroBR.md)
  : Hospital admissions from gastroenteritis in Brazil
- [`harmonic_block()`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/harmonic_block.md)
  [`har()`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/harmonic_block.md)
  : Structural blocks for seasonal trends and regressions
- [`intervention()`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/intervention.md)
  : An auxiliary function for model intervention
- [`joint_prior()`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/joint_prior.md)
  : Joint prior
- [`kdglm()`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/kdglm.md)
  : Fitting kDGLM models
- [`Multinom()`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/Multinom.md)
  : Multinom outcome for kDGLM models
- [`noise_block()`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/noise_block.md)
  [`noise()`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/noise_block.md)
  : noise_block
- [`Normal()`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/Normal.md)
  : Normal outcome for kDGLM models
- [`noticeSARI`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/noticeSARI.md)
  : SARI data from Belo Horizonte
- [`plot(`*`<dlm_coef>`*`)`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/plot.dlm_coef.md)
  : Visualizing latent states in a fitted kDGLM model
- [`plot(`*`<fitted_dlm>`*`)`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/plot.fitted_dlm.md)
  : Visualizing a fitted kDGLM model
- [`Poisson()`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/Poisson.md)
  : Poisson outcome for kDGLM models
- [`polynomial_block()`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/polynomial_block.md)
  [`pol()`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/polynomial_block.md)
  : Structural blocks for polynomial trends and regressions
- [`regression_block()`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/regression_block.md)
  [`reg()`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/regression_block.md)
  : Structural blocks for regressions
- [`simulate(`*`<fitted_dlm>`*`)`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/simulate.fitted_dlm.md)
  : Draw samples from the distribution of the latent states
- [`smoothing()`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/smoothing.md)
  : Auxiliary function for model smoothing
- [`specify(`*`<dlm_block>`*`)`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/specify.dlm_block.md)
  : Specify method for dlm blocks
- [`summary(`*`<fitted_dlm>`*`)`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/summary.fitted_dlm.md)
  : Summary for a fitted kDGLM model
- [`TF_block()`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/tf_block.md)
  [`AR()`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/tf_block.md)
  [`TF()`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/tf_block.md)
  : Structural blocks for auto regressive trends and regressions
- [`update(`*`<fitted_dlm>`*`)`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/update.fitted_dlm.md)
  : update.fitted_dlm
- [`zero_sum_prior()`](https://silvaneojunior.github.io/kDGLM/reference/zero_sum_prior.md)
  : Zero sum prior
